HEXAWARE : U.S. COURT DISMISSES MAJOR PATENT CLAIMS IN $500 MN LAWSUIT ⚖️

• 🇺🇸 U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Illinois dismissed the federal patent-related claims filed by Natsoft Corporation and Updraft LLC against Hexaware. 

• 🚫 Court held that the asserted patents relate to broad, abstract ideas and are not eligible for patent protection under U.S. law. 

• 📄 The lawsuit had originally alleged patent infringement and breach of contract, with an initial claim of USD 500 million. 

• ⚖️ Following dismissal of federal patent claims, the Court also dismissed related state-law claims, including contractual and business claims. 

• 🔬 Hexaware stated its Amaze®, Tensai® and RapidX® platforms are built on proprietary engineering and that the company itself holds U.S. patents for its technologies. 

• ⏳ Plaintiffs have been granted time to file an amended complaint with a new federal claim. 

• 💼 Company said the litigation has caused no material impact on operations, customer commitments, partner programs or financial position. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (A major portion of the $500 million litigation has been dismissed, reducing legal overhang and uncertainty for Hexaware.)

OECD commercial oil stocks are unlikely to reach very high levels following sharp 2026 draws

Security premium compensating for disruption risk is likely to keep a floor under prices.

SEBI Proposes Common Pricing Rules To End Stock Price Gaps Across Exchanges

India's market regulator, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI), has proposed a new mechanism to ensure the same stock does not trade at widely different prices across exchanges, seeking to improve price discovery and liquidity in thinly traded shares.

Under a consultation paper released Thursday, SEBI proposed that when a stock fails to trade on one exchange but trades on another, the inactive exchange should use the active exchange's closing price to determine the next day's pre-open base price and price band.

At present, exchanges independently calculate circuit limits using their own previous closing prices. In illiquid stocks, prolonged periods of non-trading on one exchange can leave prices and circuit limits frozen, even as the same stock continues to move on another platform. The divergence can eventually restrict trading and create two different market prices for the same security.

SEBI said the proposal is aimed at addressing such anomalies and ensuring uniform price bands for stocks listed on multiple exchanges.

The regulator has also proposed that where a stock trades on more than one exchange but remains inactive on another, the inactive exchange should adopt the closing price of the venue with the highest trading volume. Exchanges would be required to establish arrangements for sharing closing prices to implement the mechanism.

The changes are expected to primarily affect illiquid and small-cap stocks that do not trade regularly across all exchanges, while having little impact on actively traded securities.

IVP LIMITED : MBPT PASSES EVICTION & DAMAGES ORDER ⚠️

• ⚖️ IVP has received an order from the Estate Officer, Mumbai Port Authority (MBPT) under the Public Premises Act, 1971 regarding a long-standing lease rent dispute. 

• 🏭 The dispute relates to a Mumbai Port Trust lease property, where IVP has challenged revised rent demands raised by MBPT since 2006. 

• 💰 Estate Officer has directed payment of: • ₹56.84 Cr as compensation (including taxes & interest) • ₹79.97 Cr as damages (including GST & interest) 

• 📊 Total claim against the company stands at ₹136.82 Cr. 

• 🏛️ IVP's writ petitions challenging the demand are pending before the Bombay High Court, with judgment reserved in one of the key matters. 

• 📑 The company plans to challenge the Estate Officer's order through statutory appeals and writ petitions before the City Civil Court and Bombay High Court. 

🟠 Impact: Negative (Large claim exposure and adverse order create legal and financial uncertainty, although the company is contesting the demand and has not acknowledged the claims as debt.)

ALMONDZ GLOBAL : ETHANOL POLICY BOOST TO BENEFIT GREEN FUEL BUSINESS 🌾⛽

• 🇮🇳 Almondz Global said the Government's decision to exempt E22-E30 ethanol blended petrol from excise duty and launch E85 fuel is expected to accelerate ethanol demand in India 

• 🏭 Through its SPV Premier Green Innovations Pvt. Ltd. (PGIPL), Almondz has exposure to grain-based fuel ethanol manufacturing 

• 📊 Almondz holds a 40.99% stake in PGIPL, while group entity Avonmore Capital holds an additional 8.88% stake 

• 🌾 PGIPL operates a 285 KLPD ethanol plant in Himachal Pradesh and has commenced commercial production at its 200 KLPD Odisha facility 

• ⛽ Higher ethanol blending targets and E85 rollout could drive increased procurement by Oil Marketing Companies, improving plant utilisation and revenue opportunities for PGIPL 

• 📈 Management believes the policy changes provide stronger demand visibility, better operating leverage and long-term growth prospects for its green fuel business 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Higher ethanol blending mandates and fiscal incentives can significantly boost demand for PGIPL's ethanol production, benefiting Almondz through its strategic stake.)

reports.text:
NLC INDIA : SIGNS MoU WITH CSIR-CECRI FOR CRITICAL MINERALS & RARE EARTHS 🤝⛏️

• 🔬 NLC India has signed an MoU with CSIR-Central Electrochemical Research Institute (CSIR-CECRI), Karaikudi for collaboration in critical and strategic mineral beneficiation and extraction technologies

• 🌍 Partnership aims to support the Government's National Critical Mineral Mission

• 🧲 Focus areas include extraction and recovery of Rare Earth Elements (REEs) and other critical minerals from overburden, lignite/coal mine waste and tailings

• 🏭 Studies will initially be carried out on materials generated from Neyveli Mines to assess recovery potential of critical minerals

• 📚 Collaboration will also explore similar opportunities across other NLC mining and exploration projects

• 🇮🇳 Initiative is aimed at strengthening India's resource security, critical mineral self-reliance and sustainable supply chains

🟢 Impact: Positive (Enhances NLC India's exposure to the strategic critical minerals sector and could unlock value from mining waste through advanced extraction technologies.)

HSBC On NALCO

Maintain Buy, TP Rs 480

Rising Bauxite pushes up Alumina

Large domestic Aluminium premium increase in 1Q

Guinea’s bauxite export curbs have driven bauxite and alumina prices higher. This is positive for NALCO

Estimate NALCO’s domestic aluminium prices are +17% as domestic premiums have been reset higher

NALCO remains a clean aluminium and alumina proxy with attractive 5.1x EV/EBITDA valuation

Jefferies On Metals

JSW Steel/TATA  Steel trading above their long-term average EV/EBITDA

High entry valuation is a key investor concern.

Last 15-year data shows that 12-month stock returns were 15-39ppt higher when entry EV/EBITDA was above long-term average vs below.

This inverse correlation arises as multiples expand in periods of depressed spreads, with anticipation of earning upgrades.

FY27-28 EPS for JSTL/TATA are 13-36% above street.

JSW Steel is top buy in metals.

Macquarie On Pharma

Divis Labs-Maintain Outperform, TP Rs 7500

Cohance- Maintain Outperform, TP Rs 1150

Syngene- Maintain Outperform, TP Rs 835

Updated DOD 1260H list good for Indian CDMOs

Signals private companies to shift their supply chains eventually

Estimate Indian CDMO sector is ~US$7bn, representing ~4% of global CDMO market

Could meaningfully accelerate Indian CDMO market growth and market size could double in the medium term 

Remain positive on  CDMO coverage companies such as Divi's Labs, Syngene and Cohance.

Macquarie On Lenskart

Maintain Outperform, TP Rs 600

Believe growth tailwinds in India seen for last two quarters are sustainable and see a path to a 35%+ Ebitda CAGR over FY26-29E.

International opportunity shows promise across sales and margin with performance to date tracking ahead of India at comparable scale.

Raise EPS/TP 5% each to factor in these tailwinds

See EPS tripling over FY26-29E.

Kotak Institutional Equities On Q-Commerce

Eternal-Retain Buy, FV Rs 385

Available metrics of top-3 QC companies suggest these companies ended FY2026 with 4,525 stores and a combined NMV of Rs277 bn in 4QFY26.

Alert-NMV is Net Merchandise Volume

The combined NMV for the three players increased 81% yoy in FY2026 compared to 84% yoy growth for Blinkit + Instamart.

Price competition remains high, with new entrants offering discounts and cash backs
Incumbents such as Blinkit seem to be more disciplined.

Near-term growth for Blinkit may revive

MORGAN STANLEY ON AI 🤖📈

• AI is still in its early stage, similar to where computers were in the 1980s and the internet was in the 1990s.

• The biggest benefits from major technologies usually come years later, after businesses fully adopt them.

• Companies are only starting to use AI to automate work, improve efficiency and make better decisions.

• While markets have already rewarded AI-related stocks, the larger productivity gains may still lie ahead.

• AI can benefit many sectors beyond technology, including healthcare, finance, manufacturing and logistics.

• If AI helps businesses produce more at lower costs, it could become a major driver of global economic growth over the coming years.

DSM FRESH FOODS (ZAPPFRESH) ANALYSIS

✅ Operates a vertically integrated farm-to-fork protein platform with presence across fresh meat, seafood and ready-to-cook/ready-to-eat foods. 

✅ Strong growth track record with revenue increasing from ₹56 Cr in FY23 to ₹221 Cr in FY26, while EBITDA margin improved from 5.5% to 14.1%. 

✅ Diversified business model with D2C, B2B, HoReCa and retail store presence. B2B now contributes 68% of revenue, improving scale and operating leverage. 

✅ Acquisitions such as Dr. Meat, Bonsaro and Ambrozia have expanded geographic reach and added high margin RTE/RTC capabilities through Meevaa Foods. 

✅ Varuna Aquatech subsidiary provides backward integration in seafood, which management expects to improve margins by 200 to 300 bps over time. 

✅ Presence in a large and largely unorganized market where nearly 90% of meat processing remains unorganized, creating significant opportunity for branded players.

The rupee’s slide against US dollar has sharpened over the past 2 years.

From Rs 83.35 per $1 in April 2024, the exchange rate moved past Rs 95 per dollar by May 2026.

At its weakest point in this period, the rupee touched Rs 96.84 per $1 on May 20, before recovering slightly to Rs 95.44 per $1 on May 26.

This means the dollar became about 16% costlier for anyone converting rupees into dollars — affecting import costs, overseas travel, foreign education payments, and companies with dollar-linked expenses.

A weaker rupee can also add pressure on India’s import bill, especially for commodities such as crude oil, while making exports relatively more competitive.

The world wants far more data center capacity than the ecosystem can build and commission fast enough. The cleanest theme: AI infrastructure bottlenecks and their beneficiaries.
